reviews.review_title: Avoid for now! Full of bugs
reviews.review_description:
I don’t write reviews like this lightly, but wait for another couple updates.
My review is now posted on my YouTube channel ‘Gotcha VR’
I’ve never felt so frustrated with a game!
It started off well, but I fell down a hole before collecting a badge I think and I couldn’t get passed the cargo area on floor 3, I can’t get back to floor 2 to get the badge, so I got stuck, I had to start the game all over again after 2 hours of playing.
On my second attempt, I just got hit with tons of bugs, my gun wouldn’t shoot the target, I restarted, then my hands wouldn’t grip the panel to pull it off to recharge the gun, I restarted, then I made an injection and it wouldn’t let go of it and put it in the a pack, I restarted, then the same happened but I was able to move on, that was until I met the captain and had to use 2 hands to mind meld with her, but my hand wouldn’t let attach to her head because it wouldn’t let go of the injection, so I gave up and tried requesting a refund, of course I was denied because I’d played longer than 2 hours trying to put up with the bugs.

developer_response.body: Hi! Thank you for your feedback. We have just released a hotfix addressing performance, stability, and UX issues on Meta Quest. Please make sure your game is updated to the latest version, and let us know if it improves your experience!

reviews.review_title: No bugs that I can see. Atmospheric!
reviews.review_description:
I'm guessing the bugs reported in all the other reviews have been fixed as I haven't experienced any on my first play for a couple of hours. It's really a shame games are launched with bugs as it really is a case of "you only get one chance to make a first impression" and people will assume the game is permanently buggy despite developer updates.

Anyway im enjoying it a lot, it's nicely polished and atmospheric!

If you have ever dreamed of truly stepping onto a Federation starship and feeling the hum of the warp core beneath your feet, Star Trek: Infection is the definitive VR experience you’ve been waiting for. This isn't just a game; it is a breathtaking realization of the Trek universe that sets a new gold standard for atmospheric storytelling in virtual reality.

The Atmosphere: Beyond the Final Frontier
From the moment you put on the headset, the sense of scale and detail is staggering. The developers have managed to capture that elusive "Star Trek feel"—balancing the clean, optimistic aesthetic of Starfleet with a palpable sense of tension and mystery. The lighting, the sound design, and the interactive consoles make the environment feel alive. You don’t just play this game; you inhabit it.

Performance and Polish
While the journey through the stars had a few "space anomalies" at launch—specifically some minor micro-stuttering—it is incredibly refreshing to see a dev team so committed to their craft. A recent patch has already significantly smoothed out these performance hiccups. It’s clear the developers are listening to the community, and the game now runs beautifully, allowing the incredible art direction to shine without distraction.

Final Verdict
Star Trek: Infection is a triumph. It’s rare to find a licensed title that treats its source material with this much respect while delivering a top-tier VR gameplay experience. Huge kudos and a massive "thank you" to the development team for creating such a beautiful, polished, and genuinely "genial" piece of software.

Whether you are a die-hard Trekkie or just a VR enthusiast looking for the next great immersive adventure, this is a mandatory addition to your library. Engage!

reviews.review_title: If you like Star Trek, I recommend
reviews.review_description:
I’ve genuinely been enjoying this game.

I am an All Trek fan though. If you’re a Star Trek fan, especially one who’s ever wanted to be inside the world and not just watch it, I’d recommend giving this a shot.

What stands out most to me is the immersion. Being able to walk the corridors, interact with the environment, use tools like the tricorder with authentic sounds…it all adds up. It’s something I’ve wanted for years. Bridge Crew gave us the bridge, but this lets you experience the rest of the ship, and that alone makes it special.

The Captain’s Logs and environmental storytelling also do a lot to make it feel like you’re stepping into an unfolding episode rather than just playing through missions.

Now, to be fair, the game did launch with some issues. Performance hiccups and a few rough edges were definitely noticeable. But even with those, I was still having fun. And more importantly, the dev team moved quickly to release a hotfix, now the experience is noticeably smoother and more enjoyable.

What I appreciate is that this feels like a foundation, not a one-off. Or at least that’s what I hope. Either way, there’s real potential here for continued development: more episodes, expanded systems, deeper immersion. If that happens, this could grow into something even more special for Trek fans and VR in general.

At the end of the day, I’m not going to knock one of the very few Star Trek VR experiences we have especially when it’s clear there’s care, effort, and responsiveness behind it.

I appreciate the details and respect the craft. Looking forward to what’s next.

LLAP 🖖🏿

reviews.review_title: Heavily story driven but lacks horror
reviews.review_description: Visually it’s just okay and so far, I’m not getting that whole horror and sneaking around experience. Yes it has a creepy atmosphere, the blood and infection spread, dead bodies, and the occasional dead infected that suddenly has a spasm. However, I was able to walk straight up around an infected and it didn’t do anything. Not till a failed attempt of using the vulkan technique did it suddenly turn around and attacked me. Initially, the game felt action packed and full of events and narrative. But once you get into the actual infected part of the story it feels like it slammed the breaks. idk it just feels like it’s lacking substance. The objectives do keep me busy but I was hoping more of dodging or sneaky by a lot of infecteds while doing so. The interrogation part and just the overall sequence of events are a. It confusing. Will say the narrating and and the amount of interactions is well done.

Some have complained about bugs but they seem to largely gone now. I haven't experienced any as yet. The game has a good atmosphere, feels Trekky and the voice acting is good too. One thing is unnecessarily finniky. The shoulder squeeze, you have to squeeze until you see a blue light and I think stop squeezing and hold to take down the zombie crew member. It's very hit and miss and the instruction isn't so clear. I only managed it by accident after trying many times. They just need to simplify that to squeeze and it takes the opponent down. OK, I got the shoulder squeeze right and it's 5 stars!

Just finished after loading the latest update. A few bugs along the way like getting stuck in crouch position or walls but nothing too frustrating. Need more save stations or maybe instant save. I always play first run on easiest difficulty to learn the play and game mechanics. Now I know what to do I'll definitely be playing again. Very satisfying as you work your way through the missions.

reviews.review_title: Still some issues, but game has improved
reviews.review_description:
I'm happy to report that one of the most glaring issues of the game, the constant jitter when moving, has been addressed! As such, I've updated my reviews to four stars, and I want to express my appreciation to the devs for their continued work after a shaky release.

I still experience the weird movement issues I described before, where it feels eight-directional as opposed to a one-to-one translation of the thumbstick angle, making movement often feel clumsy and jerky. And it still bugs me that, because I'm physically shorter than the player character, the game always has me moving around at a slightly crouch. Changing to "Sitting" mode (even though I'm still standing) looks like it may alleviate the problem somewhat, but a height calibration option would indeed be welcome. There's also still some weirdness when manipulating objects. Sometimes my hands get stuck in a table or something, and it just feels kind of clumsy overall.

All that said, the game has a great atmosphere, and I think I can start enjoying the story now without quite so much annoyance at its technical imperfections. Thank you again to the devs, and I hope further improvements are forthcoming!

reviews.review_title: Great game with room for improvement
reviews.review_description:
(I absolutely LOVE everything about the concept. The story, characters and the ship's design are just amazing. BUT I don't think the game was completely ready to be launched yet. Bugs, glitches and missing tutorials lead to a lot of confusion during the game play. 
Great game, but there's still a lot of room for improvement. So 3/5 for now.)
EDIT:
When I asked for help on Discord they immediatly fixed my game. With every update there are fewer bugs. The game keeps getting better, keep it up!

This came out of nowhere but it's great! Visually one of the prettiest game for quest 3 and still no loading screen! Stealth and generally gameplay is so much fun! But first of all - narration is mind-blowing :o no spoilers but... just play :)

I have not encountered blocking issues and was able to finish the game with latest updates. I loved it despite few glitches like sliders sometimes not moving (try diff distances). Also when I exited at room puzzle, it failed to keep door open so needed to start over (but there's now autosave). The Vulcan neck pinch worked fine for me (hold trigger until it's blue and then keep it at that point by alternating quickly releasing and holding trigger. Picking up stuff works alright (object could get stuck but you can retry). I had an issue now and then that knees were bent and as workaround went to settings and lowered floor level to compensate. However don't see that issue always so might be Quest issue. A few things are trial and error since tutorial is not always clear. I.e. the hypospray requires you to hold it and not just push once. Second connecting grappling hooks can only activate one at a time (and you need to continue from there to connect to the next one). Isolinear spanner has a knob to rotate either left/right. Left is to erase the path in case you messed up created crossing paths.  Graphics are decent and story keeps evolving with frequent character interactions. The game also has a bit of variety which keeps it interesting. Framerate drops sometimes but since not an action game I can live with that.
This is mostly a puzzler with an evolving story and not an action shooter game (stealth & utilizing distraction with strategy/gadgets with limited shooting). I did see only few jump scares (always same scene) but didn't find it too scary since you know what to expect after a while. Focus is on navigating the ship, solving puzzles & deal with infected crew. Difficulty is average and they could have required craft station bit more. Story is like a good episode and note there are 2 alternate endings (reload to see both). For Star Trek it would be nice if this was on the well-known larger starships with well-known characters but still feels Star Trek to me.

I love Star Trek, and wanted to try this game. I won a key, and was very excited! I have never played this typeof game before, so I chose Journey mode. I knew that I was taking a chance that I would be lost. I will say, and I believe this goes for many complex adventure/survival games, that more direct instruction maybe in a separate place for total beginners would be really helpful. I had my son start the game and cast it to phone and he pointed out many of the gameplay mechanics that for many would be obvious and need no explanation. That said, once I got the hang of it, I am really enjoying the gameplay. I killed a zombie! Ok, it took 2 tries, but still cool. Am going to enjoy being terrified and hopefully go on to play more of these games. Thanks to the developers for the nudge that is satisfying my trekkie nerd side.

I'm a huge Star Trek fan, have seen every episode of every series, purchased every game available on every platform I've ever owned. After 3 patches I thought it would be safe to start playing this game, but in my first 2 hours I found myself constantly being stuck in a crouched position(consequently unable to run). While the tricorder works as might be expected, the phaser can only fire in extremely short burst that appear to last a frame or two before disappearing. There is no sweeping with a phaser or moving it at all after its been fired and the overall beam could be missed with a hiccup/framedrop and frequently is. It just doesn't feel like I'm firing a star trek phaser as depicted in games such as Star Trek Voyager: Elite Forces I/II.

Being stuck on the floor, unable to run, and dying from a single strike from the enemy was not a great introduction to what could be a great game. If I play Bridge Crew I'm reminded how much better Bridge Commander is for gameplay, and similarly this has me remembering the large interactive starship with functional holodecks and proper phaser fire in Elite Forces and wondering why this came out the way it did.

The neck pinch and mind meld mechanics work well, and perhaps suggest this game is best played leaning into the stealth and non-lethal mechanics.

It also isn't clear when a collectible is, well, collected. I am notified an item is in an area, but picking it up does nothing. Scanning seems to get rid of the notification, but I haven't collected anything in the way I can collect resources or items in the game.

Probably best to wait until further updates. I'll update my review if/when the situation is improved.

reviews.review_title: Boldly go with the right expectations
reviews.review_description:
About 38% in and am totally sucked into the game. The game nails atmosphere - its narrative, visuals, and sound design all work together to create a steadily intensifying sense of unease. Being a Star Trek fan adds to the experience, but the story stands up strong enough on its own.
 
There’s a bit of a learning curve early on and some mechanics (like neck pinch and mind meld) can feel awkward at first, but once mastered, the controls are simple. There are ‘meditation’ learning guides to help with the key stuff and the in-game comms trail is a smart touch for navigating the ship’s maze like layout. If you’re still stuck, checking a quick walkthrough helps. 
 
Performance wise, it seems like the developers ironed out early issues. I waited a bit before jumping in and haven’t encountered any glitches so far with the experience feeling relatively polished and very immersive. This isn’t a jump scare horror game and leans more into eerie, psychological tension rather than outright terror. If you’re expecting fast-paced zombie chaos like Arizona Sunshine, this isn’t that. The pacing is slower and more narrative-driven, though things pick up once the infection mechanics come into play.
 
Overall, I’d definitely recommend it, just go in expecting a story rich, atmospheric VR experience rather than full-on action horror.

reviews.review_title: Worst VR game I’ve played - refunded
reviews.review_description: This is such a shame. This game should’ve never been released in this god awful state. The movement is not smooth, it’s extremely juddery even after TWO updates that apparently addressed this. Even moving your arms is juddery and not smooth. What a shame, it also looks like an early 2018 VR game. We are now in 2026, look at games like Red Matter 2 - we can do better than this. Games like this stop VR from moving forward. I’m a huge Star Trek fan and this should’ve been brilliant. It is not. DO NOT BUY.

developer_response.body:
We’re sorry to hear you didn’t have a good experience with the game.

Since your refund, we’ve released our latest patch, which includes improvements to smoothness and overall performance. These issues have now been addressed, and the experience should be noticeably better.

We completely understand your frustration, especially as a Star Trek fan, and we’re sorry the game didn’t meet expectations at the time. If you decide to give it another try in the future, we believe you’d have a much better experience now.

reviews.review_description:
I bought it the day it came out. I've never been a fan of Star Trek, but big titles like this are always good, and it didnt disappoint. I did run into a few bug tiny bugs. It was always during tutorials. I just closed the game and opened back up and it fixed it. There were saves everywhere so I didn't lose much progress. The graphics were cool. It made me kinda want to check out Star Trek. The game is very long, so you get your moneys worth and then some. It's definitely a 5 Star game. It took me about 3 days of alot of gameplay to complete. Once I completed it, I started over to collect everything I had missed the first time and complete all the challenges, (the second time i didnt run into any bugs). I love games with side activities.
I was upset it didn't come out in December, but it was worth the wait. I'm sad it's over.

The devs sent me an update about a hotfix that was supported to correct the stuttering issues. Unfortunately this hotfix doesn't appear to work, and the stuttering continues. It's particularly bad when moving your arms, or trying to use the tricorder. I will keep my review at 2 star until this issue is fixed. 

I really wanted to like this game, as I'm a huge 90's Trek fan, but so far I've been disappointed because of poor performance. Clearly this game wasn't tested thoroughly enough. Playing on a Quest 3 128GB.

reviews.review_title: Has potential but performance is poor
reviews.review_description:
First off, big Star Trek fan, grew up on TOS and was hooked since. This is a game made by fans. The bones of something good is here and I really hope they can sort performance out, but right now there is a distinct stuttering with moving your hands and hitches when moving around. Even in some of the tutorial sections you can feel the game struggling with consistent frame rates. The game needs tweaking, and I feel it needed more play testing too. I will admit this has been on my wish list since it was announced, and I was 
really looking forward to it, but right now I was tempted to refund it. My advice wait until there’s been a few patches to iron out the very obvious bugs.

developer_response.body: Hi! Thank you for your feedback. We have just released a hotfix addressing performance, stability, and UX issues on Meta Quest. Please make sure your game is updated to the latest version, and let us know if it improves your experience!

reviews.review_title: Boldly Going Where VR Hasn’t Gone Before
reviews.review_description:
I have only dropped in enough to do a first impressions review.  From the moment you step in, it feels like you’re part of a high-stakes sci-fi episode, with immersive environments, strong audio design, and a storyline that keeps you engaged throughout. Controls are intuitive and visually the game does a great job within the native Quest’s capabilities, with detailed environments and a real sense of being aboard a starship. The sound design—especially the ambient ship noises and voice work—really helps sell the experience.  I have not noticed the stuttering other players have but it has been patched recently.
An absolute must-play for fans of Star Trek and anyone looking for a great narrative-driven game, and one I see the devs are quickly updating and patching to give the best experience makes it a 5 star game.

reviews.review_title: Good game , but too hard even on easy
reviews.review_description: I Like this game and the graphics are amazing. But it needs a lot of improvement. For example, the nerve pinch is impossible to do. No matter how you follow the instructions.No matter how many times you do it , you can't do the nerve pinch.  Then there's this manner issue. The first time I did it. It took me forever but I finally got all the connections to work. Going and I kept getting killed by a gigantic flower monster could not get past it so figuring, I make a mistake, I went back through the door only to find The door was no longer repaired, so I had to repair the door to get back in the room again and now I can't repair it because the spanner doesn't work.  No matter how hard I try, I cannot get the little red line to connect. That is ridiculous. There is no reason for it to be this hard. And it does not make it more interesting to make it this hard.  I recommend to anybody and everybody eats this wait until all the bugs are fixed because it is not worth the twenty nine dollars to get stuck in a low level on easy

developer_response.body:
Thanks for sharing this feedback, and we’re really sorry to hear your experience was so frustrating.

We’ve made a number of tweaks in the latest patches, including improvements to the nerve pinch, progression issues, and overall difficulty balancing. Those sections should now feel much smoother and less punishing than before.

We really appreciate you taking the time to point this out, and we’re sorry again that your earlier experience didn’t reflect what we want the game to feel like. It should be in a much better place now.

reviews.review_description: A truly Star Trek experience. The atmosphere of the Star Trek: Deep Space 9 series is palpable at every turn. The game is story-driven. This Star Trek horror stealth game is a must-have not only for fans.

reviews.review_title: A great Star Trek adventure. Great game!
reviews.review_description:
I had been waiting for this game for a long time, and I was not disappointed. The visuals are stunning, and it’s truly enjoyable to walk around inside the ship. The gameplay is also very interesting and engaging.

It’s true that, as many people have mentioned, there are some bugs—especially for those like me who started playing right when the game was released—but for me, it did not ruin the overall experience.

One thing I’m not sure everyone noticed: in the main menu, where you are on the ship’s bridge, you can actually move around and walk across the bridge. This really surprised me, and it’s a lot of fun.

My request to the game developers: please expand this feature and allow free roaming throughout the entire ship, using the tricorder and the in-game map. It’s really enjoyable and could be a great addition even after finishing the main story.

Thank you to the company that developed the game and to the development team for creating an experience that truly feels like being in the Star Trek universe.

Based on my full playthrough, I feel some of the comments in the reviews are a little unfair. Sure there are few glitches here and there but it's a solid sci-fi game with one of the best stories I've played in VR and fantastic voice acting. There's some innovation here and welcome weirdness. It's dark but not horror. Make sure you play around with all of the craftables, it adds to the experience. I probably spent around 10 hours or more and it entertained me to the end. Worth the four stars.

reviews.review_title: Awesome! Not ST focused, but so what?
reviews.review_description: Sure, there are some rough edges and it is more a story in the ST universe rather than a ST focused story, but IMHO a lot of the negative reviews are unfair. If you like SciFi an have played other adventure games you'll do just fine and have a great time.

I want to love this but for an amateur VR player that death grip is impossible I don’t understand how it works so frustrating I’ll have to wait for an update and hope it’s more clear later and come back to the game maybe some video tutorials on YouTube?

Looks good to start but too many bugs.  Game progress doesn’t save properly, many times tutorials don’t work in the game, too many ‘no escape, no way to proceed’ issues. Items collected (over the shoulder) aren’t stored.  Far too frustrating after starting three new games just to see if I could proceed.  
Developer has a LOT of work to do

developer_response.body: Thanks for your feedback. The issues you mentioned have been addressed in the latest update, including save progress, tutorial flow, item storage, and several progression blockers. We appreciate you taking the time to report them, and we hope the game experience is now much smoother.

reviews.review_description: I'm about halfway through now and the game is good, just wanted to update my review to recommend the game and let people know my issues are gone.

So Ive made it a bit past the bridge but honestly cant play further due to the constant framerate drops. Also when climbing the first ladder about halfway up all the graphics cut out. Other than that it seems like a pretty basic zombie game with a Star Trek skin so far

developer_response.body: Thanks for sharing your feedback. A recent patch addressed the performance issues you mentioned, including the framerate drops and the graphical problem when climbing the first ladder. It should be a much smoother experience now.

reviews.review_description:
I actually think the game is great. It's the first game I've played where I don't get motion sick without using teleportation. It's exciting and not too scary.
Edit: I found the mistake. It was my fault.

developer_response.body:
Hi ChrisDoro98,
Are you playing on Normal or Journey difficulty?
Did you try completing the full infection minigame, so that when you get fully infected, you have a bit of time to bite off the eyes from your forearms, and then your infection level lowers so you can infect yourself once again?
Please send us a video of you issue to support@playedwithfire.com so that we can help you out

reviews.review_description: Playing after the hotfix dropped and haven't had issues. Love the inclusion of Quest Achievements as we are tracking them on the new Meta Achievement Hunting App! Gameplay is solid and while I wasn't much of a Star Trek kid growing up, it's a lot of fun to be in this universe.

reviews.review_title: Fantastic thrill ride and epic adventure
reviews.review_description: Fabulous thrill ride and epic adventure so far after about 2 hrs , Im loving it . Don't pay any attention to the 1 star reviews, my guess they are trying to play on a q2 and the game is not designed for the quest 2 , its for the 3 and 3s only. Patch is coming out to fix small bugs which haven't bother me at all to this point.

Some bugs on S3. Have to exit game and go back in to remove pop ups in order for things to work. But not bad game. Loving the story. Seems like even though I reset floor height, my characters legs are bent a bit. No setting to auto hold phasers or tricorder. Can’t seem to find a game exit back to meta quest home. I am enjoying the game.

developer_response.body: Hi! Thank you for your feedback. We have just released a hotfix addressing performance, stability, and UX issues on Meta Quest. Please make sure your game is updated to the latest version, and let us know if it improves your experience!

reviews.review_description: I liked this game even tho im not a star trek fan. This game immerses you in a world and it makes you want to learn more about it. I loved this game

I don't know what's going on with quest 3 games lately. Terrible stuttering! Doesn't feel smooth at all. Ruins the entire experience. I'm real disappointed. Unplayable for me at the moment. Meta needs to do something to fix this issue. This is happening with multiple new game releases. Games are running worse then ever before.

Updated review: They just released a patch. The game plays much smoother then it did on release date. I gave it a couple more stars since I'm still early on in the game. I do like the story and atmosphere thus far. If I'm able to complete the game without any more hiccups I'll revisit the review for a final verdict. Definitely see an upgrade after the latest patch.

developer_response.body:
Thanks so much for updating your review - we really appreciate you coming back to share your experience after the patch.

We’re glad to hear the game is running more smoothly for you now and that you’re enjoying the story and atmosphere so far. We also wanted to mention that the latest patch included additional quality-of-life improvements, including more fixes related to stuttering.

Thanks again for your patience and support. We hope your experience continues to improve as you progress, and we’d be happy to hear your final verdict once you’ve spent more time with the game.

reviews.review_description:
I was so excited for this game, I have been looking forward to this for months, The opening scene as you climb the ladder to the outside of the spaceship got me giddy.
I am loving what I have played so far, the tutorial is very informative & helps you get to grips with the game.
Star Trek fans will absolutely fall in love with this game.
Story driven adventure games are my go to games.
I even got beamed up….brilliant.
I will add more about my journey as I go further into this game. 
But my anticipation has been justified, thanks devs, really appreciate the efforts in making this game.

Every time I started this game, I'd put my phaser away, yet my index finger still acted like it was on the trigger. Permanently. The result? I was micturating phaser fire at random walls during delicate stealth segments while both hands were fungus. Oh, and the wrist eyeballs disappeared on me too, so I couldn't change back to hands. That's but a tiny taste of the game breaking bugs that await you should you attempt to play this. Oh, and what's with the weird moobs and crooked nostrils on the Cardassian merchant? Rather tough to geek out over encountering a Spoonhead in VR when you're distracted by easily preventable rigging deformities. Seriously, nobody caught that?

developer_response.body:
Thanks for the detailed feedback. The latest patch has already fixed some of the issues you mentioned, and we’re still actively working on additional improvements and bug fixes.

As for the crooked nostrils, that part is actually intentional — it’s there to stay faithful to Star Trek design.

reviews.review_description: Just a minor fix needed. The visual that shows instructions to use items appears in the lower left and is out of my view. I mainly see out of my right eye, so I can't see the instructions. If possible, please move this to be more to the right and more out of the way.
I like this game, intriguing story, nice graphics. Highly recommended
After a few updates, the game runs smoothly.

reviews.review_title: Can't even get to the title screen!
reviews.review_description: Doesn’t work, can’t get any answers to fix. Negative 5 stars. Games should be able to be played. I feel as though games that you purchase should be somewhat usable or function to some degree.

developer_response.body: If you're still having trouble launching the game, please contact us at support@playedwithfire.com — we’ll be happy to help.

reviews.review_description: So far it stutters bad. Meta quest 3 512gb

developer_response.body: Hi! Thank you for your feedback. We have just released a hotfix addressing performance, stability, and UX issues on Meta Quest. Please make sure your game is updated to the latest version, and let us know if it improves your experience!

reviews.review_description: Now that the game runs without stuttering, it's worth five stars.

I'd like to ask devs about cross-buy between Quest3 and Meta Link PC (like Metro and some another games). Are you going to do it? It'll be really good and convenient for gamers, who prefers meta link and oculus, not Steam.
There's no detailed instructions on how to gather objects,  no instructions on how to play.

developer_response.body:
Thanks for your feedback - we’re sorry to hear that.

There is a guided quest at the beginning that teaches how to gather and use objects, but it seems it might not be clear enough, so we’ll definitely work on improving that.

We’d love to help you directly - feel free to reach out to us at support@playedwithfire.com or join our Discord

reviews.review_description: Great game! I’m enjoying it a lot. Wish it had chapter selection.
Just don’t work why bring games out if not playable

developer_response.body: Hi! Thank you for your feedback. We have just released a hotfix addressing performance, stability, and UX issues on Meta Quest. Please make sure your game is updated to the latest version, and let us know if it improves your experience!

reviews.review_title: Issue not working all I see is a light
reviews.review_description: So disappointed all I get is a flashing light
Interesting title trying to be something more than most of current VR games. Definitely worth playing

reviews.review_title: Exploring a star ship is fun
reviews.review_description:
Huge Trek fan here
so far i’m very immersed and impressed

the iso linear chips were a little glitchy but otherwise off to a solid start
will update my review as i get deeper into the game
